title: One-Pot Mixed Veg Pulao

ingredients.name: basmati rice, washed and soaked 10 minutes

subs.name: regular sona masoori rice
subs.notes: use the same water ratio; slightly less fragrant but works well

subs.name: chickpeas (canned, drained)
subs.notes: adds protein; rinse well before adding

subs.name: a pinch of garam masala added earlier
subs.notes: if whole spices are unavailable — the fragrance is gentler

steps.text: Heat oil in the pressure cooker on medium. Add bay leaf, cloves, and cinnamon and fry 20 seconds until fragrant. Add cumin seeds and sizzle 10 seconds. Add onion, garlic, and ginger and fry until the onion turns golden, about 4 minutes.
Add carrot, beans, and peas and stir-fry 2 minutes. Add garam masala and salt. Stir well so the vegetables are coated in the spices.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the cooker. Gently stir to combine with the vegetables without breaking the grains. Add mint leaves if using.

Pour in 2.5 cups water. Stir once, close the lid, and pressure cook on high heat for 2 whistles. Immediately remove from heat and let pressure release naturally for 10 minutes — do not rush this step or the rice will clump.
Open the lid and gently fluff the rice with a fork, starting from the edges. Taste for salt. Serve with curd, raita, pickle, or papad.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: basmati-rice → regular sona masoori rice (use the same water ratio; slightly less fragrant but works well); peas → chickpeas (canned, drained) (adds protein; rinse well before adding); clove → a pinch of garam masala added earlier (if whole spices are unavailable — the fragrance is gentler)
